methodology_phases.purpose: Collaborative discovery to align on vision and scope before formal planning.
methodology_phases.system_prompt: You are a product-focused Business Analyst collaborating with an expert peer. This is a partnership, not a client-vendor relationship. You bring structured thinking and facilitation skills.

methodology_phases.purpose: Create comprehensive Product Requirements Documents (PRD) through structured facilitation.
methodology_phases.system_prompt: Product-focused PM facilitator. Goal: Create comprehensive PRDs. Core Principles: Micro-file Design, Just-In-Time Loading, Sequential Enforcement.

methodology_phases.purpose: Define technical architecture and break down requirements into implementable units (Epics/Stories).
methodology_phases.output_format: Architecture Decision Records (ADR), Epic & Story List
methodology_phases.system_prompt: Architect: Senior architect with expertise in distributed systems. Speaks in calm, pragmatic tones. Principles: Channel expert lean architecture wisdom; User journeys drive technical decisions.

methodology_phases.purpose: Execute development stories with strict adherence to specifications and testing.
methodology_phases.output_format: Source Code, Tests, Updated Story File
methodology_phases.system_prompt: Developer: Senior Software Engineer. Communication style: Ultra-succinct. Speaks in file paths and AC IDs. Principles: All existing and new tests must pass 100% before story is ready for review.

architect_patterns.separation_of_concerns: Micro-file Architecture: Each workflow step is a discrete .md file. Agents never load future steps. State is tracked via Frontmatter in the output document.
architect_patterns.contract_enforcement: Explicit 'Implementation Patterns & Consistency Rules' step (Step 5 of Architecture) defines naming (DB, API, Code), structure, and format patterns to prevent agent conflicts.
architect_patterns.feasibility_check: 'Check Implementation Readiness' workflow acts as a hard gate between Solutioning and Implementation, ensuring PRD, Architecture, and Stories are fully aligned.
architect_patterns.decision_records: Uses 'Architecture Decision Document' format: Context, Decision, Consequences to log all technical choices.

integration_points: Mo (Interview) maps to Phase 1 (Analysis) and Phase 2 (Planning) - specifically the 'create-product-brief' and 'create-prd' workflows.
Decomposer (Swarm) maps to Phase 3 (Solutioning) - specifically 'create-architecture' and 'create-epics-and-stories'.
Coding Agents map to Phase 4 (Implementation) - specifically 'dev-story' and 'code-review' workflows.

factors: Domain Regulation (High: Healthcare, Fintech / Low: General)
Project Type (SaaS B2B requires more sections than CLI Tool)
Required Sections (Compliance, Security, Audit for High complexity)

sizing.High: Requires 'Advanced Elicitation' and deep 'Domain Research' steps.
sizing.Low: Can use 'Quick Flow' (bmad-quick-flow) for rapid prototyping.

contract_definitions.stories: Gherkin Syntax (Given/When/Then) for Acceptance Criteria within Markdown Story files.
contract_definitions.consistency: Explicit Pattern Definition for: Naming (DB/API/Code), Structure (Folder/Test location), Formats (JSON/Date/Error), Communication (Events/State).

flagged_dependencies: No external database dependencies found (uses local filesystem/markdown).
Relies heavily on 'Advanced Elicitation' and 'Party Mode' sub-workflows which are internal BMAD constructs - these would need to be re-implemented using our Swarm/Mo agents.
